name: Reconquest of Jerusalem
significance: Saladin showed mercy where Crusaders had massacred. No revenge killings.

name: Battle of Hattin
significance: Saladin destroyed Crusader army. Jerusalem retaken. True Cross captured.

name: Fall of Baghdad to Mongols
significance: Hulagu destroyed the Abbasid caliphate. Libraries in the Tigris. Civilization-ending event.

causes.note: Mongol destruction of Baghdad prompted their invasion of Islamic lands, leading to confrontation at Ain Jalut

name: Assassination of Qutuz
significance: Baybars killed Qutuz on return from Ain Jalut. Took the sultanate.

name: Battle of Ain Jalut
significance: First major Mongol defeat. Qutuz and Baybars saved Islamic civilization.

causes.note: Victory at Ain Jalut led directly to Baybars assassinating Qutuz upon return to seize power

name: Mansa Musa's Hajj
significance: So much gold distributed it crashed markets in Cairo. Put Mali on world map.

name: Fall of Constantinople
significance: Mehmed II conquered the unconquerable city. End of Byzantine Empire. New era begins.

name: First Battle of Panipat
significance: Babur defeated Ibrahim Lodi. Founded the Mughal Empire with 12,000 troops vs 100,000.

name: Fall of Timbuktu to Moroccans
significance: Moroccan army destroyed Songhai Empire. Scholars scattered. Libraries lost.

name: Battle of Seringapatam
significance: Tipu Sultan's last stand. British conquest of southern India secured.

name: Battle of Khartoum
significance: The Mahdi's forces took Khartoum. Gordon killed. British humiliated.

name: Battle of Badr
significance: First major Muslim military victory — 313 vs 1000. Defined the community.

causes.note: Quraysh sought revenge for their defeat at Badr

name: Battle of Uhud
significance: Near-defeat that tested faith. Hamza martyred. Archers disobeyed.

causes.note: Quraysh emboldened by near-victory, formed larger coalition against Medina

name: Battle of the Trench
significance: Siege of Medina. Salman's trench strategy. Coalition collapsed.

causes.note: Coalition's failure shifted dynamics toward negotiated settlement

name: Treaty of Hudaybiyya
significance: Diplomacy that looked like defeat but opened the conquest.

causes.note: Peace terms provided framework for bloodless entry into Mecca

name: Battle of Mu'tah
significance: First Muslim-Byzantine clash. Jafar martyred. Khalid's tactical retreat saved the army.

causes.note: Byzantine encounter preceded Mecca conquest, tested military capability

name: Conquest of Mecca
significance: Bloodless conquest. General amnesty. Khalid's conversion beforehand.

causes.note: Mecca's unification of Arabia created foundation for succession question

name: Battle of Yamama
significance: Decisive Ridda Wars battle. Musaylima killed. Many Quran memorizers martyred — led to Quran compilation. Nusayba lost her hand.

causes.note: Loss of Quran memorizers at Yamama led to compilation under Uthman, creating resentment that contributed to his siege

name: Death of the Prophet
significance: The event that created the succession crisis.

causes.note: Lack of clear succession mechanism led to disputes over leadership
Prophet's death triggered succession crisis and tribal rebellions

name: Ridda Wars
significance: Abu Bakr reunited Arabia. Khalid commanded. Musaylimah killed.

causes.note: Battle of Yamama was the decisive engagement of the Ridda Wars campaign

name: Battle of Qadisiyyah
significance: Destruction of Sassanid field army. Opened Persia to Islam.

causes.note: Sassanid collapse weakened regional resistance to Islamic expansion

name: Battle of Yarmouk
significance: Decisive defeat of Byzantium. Khalid's tactical masterpiece. 6-day battle.

causes.note: Byzantine defeat at Yarmouk enabled rapid Islamic advance toward Jerusalem

name: Conquest of Jerusalem
significance: Umar received keys personally. Refused to pray in Church of the Holy Sepulchre.

causes.note: Khalid's victories culminated in Jerusalem; Umar then removed him from command

name: Dismissal of Khalid
significance: Umar dismissed Khalid at peak of power. Khalid obeyed without revolt.

name: Plague of Amwas
significance: Devastating plague killed Abu Ubayda and ~25,000 soldiers. Umar ordered withdrawal but Abu Ubayda chose to stay with his men.

name: Conquest of Egypt
significance: Amr ibn al-As conquered Egypt for the caliphate. Founded Fustat (old Cairo). Became governor.

name: Battle of the Camel
significance: First Muslim civil war battle. Aisha vs Ali. Companions killing companions.

causes.note: First civil war established precedent for companion conflict; Ali's authority challenged again

name: Siege of Uthman
significance: Rebels besieged Caliph Uthman in his house for 40+ days. His murder fractured the ummah permanently. Marwan's forged letter is the proximate cause per scholarly consensus.

causes.note: Uthman's murder fractured the ummah and triggered civil conflict including the Battle of the Camel

name: Battle of Siffin
significance: Ali vs Muawiyah. Qurans raised on spears. Arbitration debacle.

causes.note: Arbitration debacle and Kharijite dissent from Siffin led to Ali's assassination

name: Assassination of Ali
significance: Kharijite assassination. End of Rashidun era. Islam's first dynasty begins.

causes.note: Ali's death shifted caliphate to Umayyads; Husayn's claim marginalized, leading to Karbala

name: Battle of Karbala
significance: Husayn martyred with 72 companions. Watershed moment. Sunni-Shia divide crystallized.

causes.note: Mukhtar's uprising directly avenged Husayn's death at Karbala by executing his murderers

name: Siege of Mecca (683)
significance: Umayyad army besieged Mecca, Ka'ba damaged by fire.

causes.note: Umayyad brutality and religious controversy delegitimized dynasty, fueling revolutionary sentiment

name: Mukhtar's Revolt
significance: Mukhtar al-Thaqafi avenged Husayn by killing his murderers.

name: Conquest of Iberia
significance: Tariq ibn Ziyad crossed Gibraltar. Visigothic kingdom fell in 2 years.

causes.note: Both represent Islamic military expansion and Christian-Muslim conflict across centuries

name: Abbasid Revolution
significance: Abu Muslim's revolution destroyed the Umayyads. Blood-soaked transition.

causes.note: Abbasid victory enabled construction of new capital as center of power

name: Founding of Baghdad
significance: Al-Mansur built the Round City. Center of the world for 500 years.

causes.note: Baghdad's prominence as civilizational hub made it target for Mongol conquest
